{{Infobox Noble | Name = Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto (武五百建命) | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|qq=}}・[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = Unknown | Renamed = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Dharma Name = | Burial Site = Believed to be {{ill|Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|森将軍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|qq=}}<ref name="jiten">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263245.html Kuno Kokuzō (Shinano)] - Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|qq=}}

{{Infobox Noble | Name = Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto (武五百建命) | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代}}・[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = Unknown | Renamed = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Dharma Name = | Burial Site = Believed to be {{ill|Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|森将軍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埴科古墳群}}<ref name="jiten">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263245.html Kuno Kokuzō (Shinano)] - Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|qq=}}
{{translated page|ja|武五百建命|version=99404174|comment=Imported full ja history}}

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|qq=}}

{{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|qid=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Q110577717|qid=Q110577717}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[Category:Has wikidata]][[d:Q87863507]] Wikidata name: [[Category:Add wikidata]] [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox Noble | Name = Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto (武五百建命) | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}・[[:en:Kofun period|Kofun period]] | Birth = Unknown | Death = Unknown | Renamed = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Dharma Name = | Burial Site = Believed to be {{ill|Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|森将軍塚古墳|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埴科古墳群|1=Hanishina Kofun group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6912108}}<ref name="jiten">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263245.html Kuno Kokuzō (Shinano)] - Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|qq=|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|qq=|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ([[:en:Nagano Prefecture|Nagano Prefecture]], [[:en:Ueda, Nagano|Ueda City]]) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, [[:en:Chikuma, Nagano|Chikuma City]], Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|qq=draft|qq=draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]] | Clan = [[:en:Ō clan|Ō clan]] (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.

In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the [[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]], Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]] and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of [[:en:Emperor Sujin|Emperor Sujin]]. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of [[:en:Kamuyaimimi|Kamuyaimimi]]<ref>“[[:en:Kujiki|Sendai Kuji Hongi]],” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.

<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武五百建命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武五百建命]] <!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[ja:武五百建命]] {{Infobox person|gender={{ill|male|WD=Q6581097}}|father={{ill|Q110577717|WD=Q110577717}}}}{{afc comment|Wikidata entry [[da:d:Q87863507]] Wikidata name: [[User:Immanuelle|Immanuelle]] ([[User talk:Immanuelle|talk]]) 09:45, 11 June 2025 (UTC)}} {{Infobox noble | Name = Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto (武五百建命) | image = | image Size = | Caption = | Era = {{ill|Ancient Japan|ja|上古|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Ancient Japan|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 古代|1=ancient history|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q41493}}・{{ill|Kofun period|en|Kofun period|WD=Q459939|lt=Kofun period}} | Birth = Unknown | Death = Unknown | Renamed = | Posthumous Name = | Deity Name = | Dharma Name = | Burial Site = Believed to be {{ill|Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja|森将軍塚古墳|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Mori Shōgunzuka Kofun|ja_comment=jawiki redirects to 埴科古墳群|1=Hanishina Kofun group|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q6912108}}<ref name="jiten">[http://www.nihonjiten.com/data/263245.html Kuno Kokuzō (Shinano)] - Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | Clan = {{ill|Ō clan|en|Ō clan|WD=Q11430816|lt=Ō clan}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}} and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}}<ref>“{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}},” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}}) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma City}}, Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

Nihon Jiten (Accessed July 6, 2018, 4:29 AM [[Japan Standard Time|JST]])</ref> | Rank = First {{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}<ref>“{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}” Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref> | Master = {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}} | Clan = {{ill|Ō clan|en|Ō clan|WD=Q11430816|lt=Ō clan}} (ancestor of the {{ill|Shinano Clan|ja|科野君|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Kanasashi Clan|ja|金刺舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kanasashi Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, {{ill|Tada Clan|ja|他田舎人直|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Tada Clan|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}}, etc.) | Parents = | Siblings = | Wife = | Children = | Notable Facts = }} '''Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto''' (武五百建命, also written as たけいおたけのみこと, たけいおたつのみこと, [[birth and death dates unknown]]) was a [[Kofun period]] [[gōzoku]] (local noble) and the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}'''. In the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, he is referred to as '''Tatei-Otake-no-Mikoto'''.
== Overview ==
According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}}, Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}} and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin}}. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi}}<ref>“{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi}},” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
== Shrines Enshrining Him ==
*{{ill|Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja|二子神社 (上田市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Futago Shrine (Ueda City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} ({{ill|Nagano Prefecture|en|Nagano Prefecture|WD=Q127877|lt=Nagano Prefecture}}, {{ill|Ueda, Nagano|en|Ueda, Nagano|WD=Q844852|lt=Ueda City}}) *{{ill|Susu Kizumi Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|須須岐水神社 (千曲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Susuki Mizugami Shrine (Chikuma City)|qq=}}, within its grounds, the Iwai Shrine (Nagano Prefecture, {{ill|Chikuma, Nagano|en|Chikuma, Nagano|WD=Q179757|lt=Chikuma City}}, Yashiro) *{{ill|Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja|唐崎神社 (千曲市)|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Karasaki Shrine (Chikuma City)|ja_comment=jawiki link invalid}} (Nagano Prefecture, Chikuma City, Amamiya)
== Notes == {{Reflist}} == See Also == *[[Kuni no miyatsuko]] *{{ill|List of People Before the Asuka Period|ja|飛鳥時代以前の人物一覧|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/List of People Before the Asuka Period|1=List of ancient Japanese figures|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q11665973}}

According to the "{{ill|Kokuzō Honki|ja|国造本紀|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kokuzō Honki|1=Kokuzo Hongi|comment=changed title based on wikidata|WD=Q133833194}}" section of the {{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}}, Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto was the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi|1=Kamuyaimimi}} and was appointed as the first '''{{ill|Kuno Kokuzō|ja|科野国造|1=Shinano no Kuni no Miyatsuko|lt=Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Kuno Kokuzō|13=User:Immanuelle/Shina no Kuni no Miyatsuko|qq=}}''' during the reign of {{ill|Emperor Sujin|en|Emperor Sujin|WD=Q333530|lt=Emperor Sujin|1=Emperor Sujin}}. Tradition holds that he founded the {{ill|Shinano Ōmiyasha|ja|科野大宮社|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Shinano Ōmiyasha|qq=}}.
== Genealogy ==
Takei-Otake-no-Mikoto is said to be the grandson of {{ill|Kamuyaimimi|en|Kamuyaimimi|WD=Q11588756|lt=Kamuyaimimi|1=Kamuyaimimi}}<ref>“{{ill|Kujiki|en|Kujiki|WD=Q2716751|lt=Sendai Kuji Hongi|1=Kujiki}},” Kokuzō Honki, Kuno Kokuzō entry.</ref>.
